title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models represent a paradigm of inference-time compute with applications to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The talk demonstrates how RLM principles can be mapped onto coding agent architectures, including through tools like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse, to solve complex tasks recursively with declarable outcomes.
writeup: This talk from AI Engineer presents Recursive Language Models (RLMs) as an inference-time compute paradigm and maps their principles onto coding agent architectures for higher performance and reliability. Raymond Weitekamp shows how the approach applies through tools including Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse, breaking complex tasks into recursive subproblems with declarable outcomes. The throughline is that recursion at test time, rather than a single pass, is the lever for tackling complex coding work.
